meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Coding Experience
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage a SaaS starter kit to overcome no coding experience. A high-impact case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid deployment.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for developers. By using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass the complexity of infrastructure and boilerplate. Combine Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding with Claude Code’s autonomous file manipulation to transform your vision into a production-ready MVP in hours, not months. Focus on business logic, not syntax.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with founders who spent six months 'learning to code' while their market window slammed shut. You don't need a Computer Science degree to ship; you need a system that translates intent into executable architecture.

This isn't about writing syntax; it's about engineering a product that generates revenue. By integrating Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you move from founder-with-a-dream to founder-with-a-deployed-solution in a single weekend.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define your SaaS data model. Focus on core entities like Users, Subscriptions, and your specific value proposition objects to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform including user authentication, Stripe subscription status, and a generic 'Project' entity with multi-tenant isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the prisma schema based on the generated antigravity output'

steps.title: Scaffolding the Core API
steps.description: Establish the backend foundation using the starter kit's patterns to handle authentication and secure API routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js API route structure for a SaaS starter kit that enforces strict RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) for all endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the auth middleware and protect the api routes'

steps.title: Building the Functional Frontend
steps.description: Leverage component libraries within the kit to build the user dashboard. Speed is the priority here, not custom CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive Tailwind dashboard layout for a SaaS app including a sidebar navigation, data table for projects, and an account settings modal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate the dashboard components using shadcn-ui and connect them to the api'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Billing
steps.description: The most critical step for monetization. Automate the checkout flow to ensure your SaaS can actually accept payments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a clean implementation for a Stripe checkout session in Next.js, including webhook handling for subscription status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add the stripe webhook listener and update the user subscription status in the database'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write integration tests for your critical paths, ensuring users can sign up and pay without friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Playwright test scripts for the user sign-up flow and the subscription checkout journey.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run the test suite and fix any identified integration bugs'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development; if the payment flow isn't validated, the product isn't a SaaS.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to maintain consistent naming conventions across your entire codebase.
Don't build custom auth; integrate a provider like Clerk or Auth0 immediately via your starter kit.
Focus on 'feature flags' early so you can deploy code to production without exposing unfinished modules to users.
Treat your starter kit as a baseline, not a cage; prune unused dependencies to keep your bundle size lean.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a feedback loop with five potential customers. Use the revenue data to determine which feature to build next, rather than guessing based on your own preferences.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt hold your business hostage. Leverage these tools to build, launch, and scale your SaaS with the precision of a veteran engineering team. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
